title: in-toto Attestation Statement
description: Schema for the in-toto Attestation Framework Statement structure. A Statement is a verifiable claim about one or more software subjects. It consists of a fixed lightweight envelope with a subject (the artifact being described) and a predicate (the claim about that subject). Common predicate types include SLSA Provenance, SPDX SBOM, and Vulnerability Scan results.

_type.description: URI identifying this document as an in-toto Attestation Framework Statement v1.

subject.description: One or more software artifacts that this statement applies to. Each subject is identified by name and cryptographic digest.

predicateType.description: URI that identifies the schema and semantics of the predicate. Well-known values include https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1 and https://spdx.dev/Document.

predicate.description: The claim about the subjects. The structure is defined by the predicateType URI. May describe provenance, vulnerability scan results, test results, code review, or any other supply chain assertion.

Subject.description: A single software artifact that is the subject of this attestation, identified by name and one or more cryptographic digests.

name.description: Human-readable name for the artifact. For files, this is typically a path or filename. For container images, this is typically an image reference without a digest.

DigestSet.description: A set of cryptographic digests for an artifact, keyed by hash algorithm name. At least one digest must be present.

sha1.description: SHA-1 hex digest. Use is discouraged for new attestations.

gitCommit.description: Full Git commit SHA-1 hash identifying a specific commit.

additionalProperties.description: Digest value for an alternate algorithm.

SLSAProvenancePredicate.description: SLSA Provenance predicate (v1) describing how an artifact was built. Used with predicateType https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1.

buildDefinition.description: Describes the inputs to the build: the build type, external parameters, and resolved dependencies.

buildType.description: URI identifying the build system type and the schema for externalParameters.

externalParameters.description: The parameters that were supplied by the caller of the build system. Structure depends on the buildType.

internalParameters.description: Parameters set by the build system itself, not controllable by the caller.

resolvedDependencies.description: External artifacts that influenced the build and are not explicitly listed in externalParameters.

runDetails.description: Details specific to this particular execution of the build.

builder.description: The build system that produced this attestation.

version.description: Version information for components of the builder.

builderDependencies.description: Artifacts that the builder used that are not part of the build definition.

invocationId.description: Unique identifier for this build invocation.

byproducts.description: Additional artifacts produced by the build that are not the primary output.

ResourceDescriptor.description: A reference to a software artifact used as input to or produced by a build. Includes a URI and optional digest and annotations.

name.description: Semver or local name used to reference this resource within the build definition.

downloadLocation.description: URI where this resource can be downloaded.

annotations.description: Additional key-value metadata about the resource.
